---
title: "Machine Learning Summer School 2026"
format:
  html:
    title-block: false
    toc: false
---

::: {.hub-hero}
# Welcome

::: {.hub-tagline}
Slides, **modules**, notebooks, and a short map of the week ‚Äî all in one place.
:::

::: {.hub-lead}
Use this site **during and after class**: open today‚Äôs deck, then try the matching notebook when you feel like coding along. If something is unclear, that‚Äôs normal ‚Äî bring it to the room or office hours. Separate lab exercises (if any) will be announced there.
:::
:::

::: {.callout-tip .hub-start appearance="simple"}
## Start here (2 minutes)

1. **Open today‚Äôs slides** ‚Äî we follow **Monday ‚Üí Tuesday ‚Üí Thursday** in class.
2. **When you‚Äôre ready**, open a **module** for a short guided page, or a **notebook** for a long IDE lab ‚Äî same R you already use (RStudio or Positron is fine).
3. **Skim ‚ÄúYour week‚Äù** below for the story of the three days ‚Äî no jargon quiz attached.
:::

::: {.hub-grid}
::: {.hub-card}
::: {.hub-pill}
Palmer Penguins
:::
### Our real-data running example

- **[About the dataset](dataset-penguins.qmd)** ‚Äî variables, biology, caveats, and how we reuse penguins **Monday ‚Üí Thursday**  
- Links to **penguin-only** notebooks from that page (mass, binary species, sex, three-class species); **`tidymodels`** walk-throughs live as **Module 04** (+ **07‚Äì08** follow-ups; see [module library](modules/index.qmd)).

Synthetic **gene** notebooks are there so we **know the true story behind the data** (which ‚Äúgenes‚Äù really matter) while we teach LASSO, ridge, and AIC.
:::

::: {.hub-card}
::: {.hub-pill}
Class slides
:::
### Follow along day by day

- **[Monday](slides/day-01-monday.qmd)** ‚Äî from questions to simple models: when a straight line (or curve) helps, and when a model is ‚Äútrying too hard.‚Äù  
- **[Tuesday](slides/day-02-tuesday.qmd)** ‚Äî branching decisions (trees), and how to keep your code organized so Thursday still makes sense.  
- **[Day 4](slides/day-04-thursday.qmd)** ‚Äî forests, boosting, neural nets, PCA/imputation in recipes, variable importance, and evaluation.  

Each deck links back here from the **footer** if you get lost.
:::

::: {.hub-card}
::: {.hub-pill}
Hands-on notebooks
:::
### Try the code yourself

- **[Notebook home](notebooks/index.qmd)** ‚Äî long `.Rmd` labs (**classical** `lm` / `glm` / `rpart` / `glmnet` on genes + penguins)  
- **`tidymodels` on penguins (site modules):** [Module 04](modules/module-04-pipeline.qmd#train-test-last-fit) (train/test, then [Tuesday pipeline](modules/module-04-pipeline.qmd#canonical-pipeline-tuesday)), [07](modules/module-07-penguins-choose-metrics.qmd) (metrics), [08](modules/module-08-penguins-compare-models.qmd) (compare RF / XGB / MLP)  
- **Genes (simulation where we know the answers):** [linear + LASSO](notebooks/linear-regression-lasso.Rmd), [logistic + tree](notebooks/logistic-regression-gene-disease.Rmd)  
- **Palmer Penguins (notebooks):** [body mass](notebooks/penguins-body-mass.Rmd), [Adelie vs Gentoo](notebooks/penguins-classification.Rmd), [sex](notebooks/penguins-sex-classification.Rmd), [three species](notebooks/penguins-species-multiclass.Rmd)  

Go as fast or slow as you like; notebooks and modules are for **you**, not for a grade on this site.
:::

::: {.hub-card}
::: {.hub-pill}
Outline & modules
:::
### If you want more structure

- **[Curriculum outline](curriculum-outline.qmd)** ‚Äî how the three days connect (also useful if you miss a session)  
- **[Module library](modules/index.qmd)** ‚Äî eight topic pages: **01‚Äì06** concepts + **04** canonical `tidymodels` pipeline (train/test beat + same include as Tuesday slides); **07‚Äì08** short **`tidymodels`** labs (metrics while tuning; compare learners). See [modules vs notebooks](modules/index.qmd#modules-vs-notebooks) on that page for how we split formats.  

Your instructors may point you to extra readings or exercises; those live wherever the course announces them.
:::
:::

::: {.hub-week}
## Your week at a glance

::: {.hub-week-intro}
Think of it as one story in three chapters: **see the pattern**, **keep your work honest**, then **choose and explain** models without pretending they‚Äôre magic. Curiosity beats perfection.
:::

- **Monday** ‚Äî predictions, when a model is ‚Äútoo wiggly,‚Äù and gentle introductions to linear models, logistic regression, and a bit of regularization. You‚Äôll see **simulated genes** (we scripted the biology so we know what‚Äôs true) and **Palmer Penguins** (real measurements) side by side.  
- **Tuesday** ‚Äî decision trees (‚Äúif bill length is above this line, go left‚Ä¶‚Äù) and how to **fit and check models in a tidy, repeatable way** in R so Thursday still makes sense.  
- **Thursday** ‚Äî stronger models, what to watch when outcomes aren‚Äôt balanced, and how to discuss **why** a model leaned one way ‚Äî without claiming it ‚Äúknows‚Äù biology or medicine by itself.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" appearance="simple"}
## For instructors & TAs

- **Local preview:** from this project folder run `quarto preview` so links resolve; after `quarto render`, you can serve `_site/` with any static file server.  

- **Course design detail** ‚Äî the [curriculum outline](curriculum-outline.qmd) describes the two-track story (genes + Palmer Penguins), calibration, causal guardrails around explainability, data/model cards, reproducibility, and assessment prompts.  
- **Site output** lives in `_site/`; GitHub Actions builds the same way for Pages.  
- **Palmer Penguins** ‚Äî [About the dataset](dataset-penguins.qmd); Tuesday‚Äôs **modeling pipeline** examples use the same **Adelie vs Gentoo** setup as the penguin classification notebook (implemented with the `tidymodels` R packages).  

- **Single source for that pipeline code** ‚Äî one shared include file drives Tuesday slides and Module 04 (`_includes/tidymodels-pipeline.qmd`); edit there only.
:::
